---
title: "Software"
bibliography: 
    - ref_numerical_optimal_control.bib
    - ref_optimal_control.bib
    - ref_model_predictive_control.bib
csl: ieee-control-systems.csl
format:
    html:
        html-math-method: katex
        code-fold: true
        code-summary: "Show the code"
crossref:
  fig-prefix: Fig. 
  eq-prefix: Eq.
engine: julia
---

The methods studied in this chapter are already quite mature and well described. Software implementations exist. Here we enumerate some of them. 

[InfiniteOpt.jl](https://infiniteopt.github.io/InfiniteOpt.jl/stable/)

[OptimalControl.jl](https://control-toolbox.org/OptimalControl.jl/stable/)

[CasADi](https://web.casadi.org/) In C++ but interfaces exist for Matlab and Python. Strictly speaking it is not a tool dedicated to optimal control, but provides symbolic expressions and automatic differentiation, which are very useful for optimal control problems. 

[acados](https://github.com/acados/acados) Implemented in C but interfaces exist for Matlab and Python [@verschuerenAcadosModularOpensource2022].

[GPOPS-II](https://www.gpops2.com/) In Matlab. Commercial.

[rockit](https://gitlab.kuleuven.be/meco-software/rockit) In Python, built on top of CasADi, interface to Matlab.
